title: Smart Sous-Vide Cooker
description: Arduino-based immersion circulator for precise water-bath cooking. A PT100 RTD probe measures water temperature; a cascaded PID loop drives a mains SSR to control a 1200 W heating element; a water pump circulates the bath. Rotary encoder and OLED provide a setpoint UI; DS3231 RTC enables cook-time countdown.
objective: Hold bath temperature within ±0.1 °C of setpoint (range 20–95 °C); UI setpoint adjustment in 0.1 °C steps via rotary encoder; cook timer 0–24 h; audible alarm at cook-end.
constraints: 230 V mains; 1200 W immersion heater; 5 V USB logic supply; safe IP44 splash-proof enclosure; ≤ £35 BOM.
supporting_fact_titles: Rotary Encoder — Incremental A/B Quadrature Pulse Counting
OLED SSD1306 Monochrome Display I²C Interface
I²C Real-Time Clock — DS3231 Timekeeping and Alarms

element_usage_notes.MAX31865 PT100 RTD Amplifier: PT100 probe epoxied into 316 SS immersion sleeve; 2-wire connection; SPI on D10–D13; polled at 5 Hz.
element_usage_notes.Solid-State Relay Mains Actuator Switchi: Fotek SSR-25DA; GPIO 5; 230 V / 25 A rated; heatsink required (1.5 W at 1 A average); zero-cross reduces EMI.
element_usage_notes.Rotary Encoder User-Interface Navigation: KY-040; CLK on D2 (INT0), DT on D4, SW on D3; 0.1 °C per detent; hold push for 1 s to start/stop cook timer.
element_usage_notes.SSD1306 OLED Display UI Module: 128×64; 3-line layout: setpoint + actual temp / cook timer countdown / PID output % + heat indicator.
element_usage_notes.DS3231 RTC Alarm Scheduling Module: Cook timer: delta-seconds tracked via RTC; alarm fires at cook-end; buzzer on D8.
element_usage_notes.Arduino Uno/Nano MCU Platform: Nano 3.0; SPI for MAX31865 (D10–D13); I²C for OLED + DS3231 (A4/A5); D5 = SSR gate.

element_interactions.description: MAX31865 SPI read every 200 ms; 4-sample rolling average applied; temperature fed as process variable into PID loop.

element_interactions.data: direction (±1 per detent), button events (short/long press) via GPIO interrupts
element_interactions.description: CW detent increments setpoint by 0.1 °C; CCW decrements; short press navigates menu; long press starts/stops cook timer.

element_interactions.data: INT falling edge at cook-end time
element_interactions.description: RTC alarm fires when cook timer reaches zero; ISR halts SSR output and triggers 5-second buzzer sequence.
PID output (0–100) maps to 1-second window: PID=60 → 600 ms GPIO 5 HIGH per second. SSR switches heater at zero-crossing each cycle.
OLED refreshed at 2 Hz; blinks 'DONE' when timer expires; bar graph shows PID output level.
